title: "podman start -a" fails to start previously created container after fresh boot
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**kind bug**

**Description**

I have a few previously created podman containers (created by "podman create ....") started by systemd services.

The systemd services were very simple, doing no more than:

```
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start -a ncserver
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop ncserver
```

On a fresh reboot, these ExecStarts would all fail

Trying to do `/usr/bin/podman start -a ncserver"` manually after a fresh reboot would provide the following error message

> unable to start container 0e0819a1019bb9d92f3dd674dd7f87e81d18e4bc0634eba955249536994d191b: container create failed: container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:424: container init caused \"apply apparmor profile: apparmor failed to apply profile: write /proc/self/attr/exec: no such file or directory\""
> : internal libpod error

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a container with "podman create"

2. Reboot

3. Attempt to start container with "podman start"

**Describe the results you received:**

Error 

> unable to start container 0e0819a1019bb9d92f3dd674dd7f87e81d18e4bc0634eba955249536994d191b: container create failed: container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:424: container init caused \"apply apparmor profile: apparmor failed to apply profile: write /proc/self/attr/exec: no such file or directory\"" : internal libpod error

Container did not start

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container should start without errors

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The problem can be worked around by doing a "podman create" or "podman run" before the "podman start"

There does not need to be any relationship between the create/run and the container you wish to start. For example, "podman run hello-world" is enough to make all failing "podman start" calls work again.

Even if this "podman create" call fails (for example, trying to create a container with a name that matches the previously created containers), all subsequent "podman run" calls will work fine

Therefore as a workaround I've added "ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman create ...." calls into my systemd unit files for each container. They normally fail as the containers already exist, but then the ExecStarts work ;)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.11.1.1
Go Version:    go1.11.1
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.11.1.1-2.1.x86_64
    path: /usr/lib/podman/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"opensuse-tumbleweed-kubic"'
    version: "20180926"
  MemFree: 12480204800
  MemTotal: 16487211008
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0~rc6-1.1.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: r2d2
  kernel: 4.19.11-1-default
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 11m 12.27s
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: btrfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Build Version: 'Btrfs v4.17.1 '
    Library Version: "102"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

physical system, openSUSE Kubic, MicroOS system role

comments.body: Thanks for providing all the details, @sysrich! Did the reboot of your node entail a new version of Podman? This would explain why the apparmor-profile can't be found anymore and is clearly a bug.
@rhatdan @mheon @baude something for 1.0. I will provide a fix.

@vrothberg no, the reboot of the node did not entail a new version of podman. Rebooting just for the sake of rebooting, with no transactional update being applied, also reproduces this problem with identical symptoms as described.
That is surprising. Could you check the AppArmor profile of the container (e.g., via `podman inspect $CTR | grep AppArmor`) and paste it here?
Seems like a second bug in the start code, where we need to load the profile as well. Thanks, @sysrich !
two bugs from one report, woo I've still got it :) You're welcome, and thanks for the fixes!

> two bugs from one report, woo I've still got it :) You're welcome, and thanks for the fixes!

We found a way to always load the latest default AppArmor profile without any manual interaction. The new release is just ahead, so the fix shouldn't be far from your Kubic boxes :-)
